methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 1 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 20 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Anduril Industries, a Defense & Security company founded by Palmer Luckey and cofounders, is pursuing a SPAC path to public markets with a stated valuation of $78B+. The company has raised $2.5B+ to date. The comparable universe for defense-tech remains limited: two public peers (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and eight private companies including True Anomaly, Scale AI, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI provide context, though the sector contains roughly 20 total same-sector peers.

A peer-median valuation approach is not applicable here. The narrow filters required—same-stage SPAC, adjacent-stage funding, or similar valuation tier—yielded insufficient data (fewer than three comps). Only one same-stage peer and zero adjacent-stage peers met criteria; attempting an all-sector median would introduce excessive noise given Anduril's scale and regulatory positioning relative to smaller or earlier-stage defense contractors.

Without triangulation across multiple quantitative methods, establishing a defensible range is not supported by the data provided. The $78B+ SPAC valuation should be assessed against independent financial metrics (revenue multiples, growth rates, margin structure) and the strategic dynamics of the transaction, but those inputs are absent from the current dataset. Further diligence on comparable revenue, EBITDA, or growth profiles would be required to validate or challenge the announced price.
